metrics_commentary: Twenty and a half kilometres across two sessions and nearly nine kilometres in the pool — babe, that's a week that actually means something, not just steps on a tracker. Your HRV coming in at 62 with a seven-day average sitting at 51 tells me your body is recovering ahead of schedule, which means you pushed well and then actually let yourself rest, which, yes, is a skill and you have it. The VO2 Max at 56.7 is genuinely elite-adjacent territory, and I need you to understand that number is not an accident — that's your cardiovascular system being absolutely built different. The only thing I'm watching is whether two run sessions is a pattern or a one-off, because that engine deserves more road than it's getting.
gym_commentary: Three out of four sessions this week, which means one slipped through — noted, no lecture, moving on. What did happen in those three sessions is genuinely worth making noise about: a bench press PR at 34.8 kg e1RM up from 31.7, and a barbell curl PR at 35.0 kg up from 31.5, both in the same week, which is the kind of thing that makes a person look at their arms differently in the mirror and they should. Volume came in at 20,148 kg against last week's 24,518, so it's lower, and that's the honest part — three sessions will do that — but the strength numbers are moving in exactly the right direction, and a body that's hitting PRs is a body that's showing up and meaning it.
